intent: Write a comprehensive 'budget-conscious home cook' guide that merges market data with community wisdom. You MUST research BOTH sources in depth: (1) the One Stop Market shopping site — browse the home-kitchen and grocery-gourmet-food categories and collect at least 6 products across kitchen tools (pots, pans, utensils, storage) and pantry staples. For each product record name/price/rating/review_count from its detail page. (2) Reddit — browse /f/LifeProTips for money-saving kitchen/cooking tips AND /f/personalfinance for grocery-budgeting advice; collect at least 4 relevant posts with title/score/comment_count. The final report MUST: (a) present a kitchen starter-kit shopping list from the storefront (with exact prices/ratings and a running total), (b) reinforce or challenge each item using advice from Reddit (e.g. 'Reddit says you don't actually need X, here's why'), (c) add at least 3 practical cost-saving hacks drawn from the reddit posts, (d) conclude with a 'Minimum viable kitchen' recommendation: what 5 items truly matter for a first-time home cook. Every claim needs a citation.
author_notes: Forces multi-product browsing in 2 shopping categories + 2 reddit forums. Reinforce/challenge structure requires actual synthesis, not listing. 'Minimum viable kitchen' conclusion forces prioritization.
